title: The Red Mist

Made by [[User:Fragmented404|Fragmented404]] {{DISPLAYTITLE:The Red Mist}} {{CSS|content= .story-text{ text-align: center; font-family: Courier New; font-size: 20px; color: #ff0000; } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: #000000; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #000000; --theme-page-text-color: #ff0000; --theme-community-header-color: #000000; --theme-border-color: #ff0000; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-accent-label-color: #ff0000; --theme-accent-color: #000000; --theme-page-accent-mix-color: #ff0000; --theme-link-color: #FF0000; } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: #000000; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #000000; --theme-page-text-color: #ff0000; --theme-community-header-color: #FF0000; --theme-border-color: #ff0000; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-accent-label-color: #ff0000; --theme-accent-color: #000000; --theme-page-accent-mix-color: #ff0000; --theme-link-color: #ff0000; } }} {{CSS|content= .fandom-community-header__image img { opacity:0; } .fandom-community-header__image::before { content:''; background:url(https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/backrooms/images/8/85/INVERTED_BOB.png/revision/latest?cb=20241005134454&format=original) no-repeat; width:97.2px; height:111.6px; background-size:88px; position: absolute; } }} {{CSS|content= a { font-family: Courier New; } }} {{Background|file=MIST.jpg}} {{Underline|FF0000}} {{Fullscreen |num=1 |show=

{{Collapse|body= |num=ProlougeI |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
Day 1 Luke woke up... Tired and confused, he does not know his surroundings. This isn't Level 9 he thought to himself He got up and looked at the window The sky, vividly chartreuse with the clouds blood orange. The grass has a dark purple hue. "It's all a dream..." he said He decided to explore... He approached the only door in the room. It's locked... He looked at the window again, night was coming already... "Tomorrow is another day," Luke said He went back to bed thinking he'd wake up back where he was... |final= {{Collapse|body= |num=DIE!!! |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
Day 2 He was wrong, this isn't a dream. He decided to pinch himself hoping that he'll return to Level 9. But nothing happened... Suddenly, he heard a faint tap on the window... He looked outside the window. Everything was covered in a crimson-red mist. He turned around and walked towards the locked door. He twisted the knob and this time it was open. He walked down the stairs until he reached the living room... Suddenly, he saw a photo he had recognized long ago. It's a photo of his former M.E.G comrades and friends, their faces were crossed out. "It's better to forget," he whispered to himself. He threw the picture to the floor, and it shattered into pieces Tomorrow is another day... |final= {{Collapse|body= |num=ProlougeII |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
Day 3 He went down the stairs to the living room... Today will be the last day. He must find an exit He grabbed a crowbar from the drawer and smashed the window, shards were flying everywhere as he peered out into the veil. He climbed out of the window and was blinded by the red veil surrounding him. He ran until he could find an exit, but there was nothing—just a dark, crimson fog. He stopped and got on his knees. Right in front of him were two corpses. He remembered both of them, they looked unmistakably like his co-workers from the picture earlier. "I'm sorry," he said softly "I'm sorry for what I've done to you. I tried to forget everything, but it isn't possible. Forgive me, Alex and Andrew, I've tried my best, but I couldn't save you. Forgive me,". He collapsed as the fog thickened to the point where he couldn't see his own hands. He picked up a pistol from one of his dead member's pockets. He points it at his head and then *clink* The gun is empty... There was nothing he could do. He can't run and he can't hide. All he can do is lie down and accept his fate. The fog thickened again; this time he could see nothing but red. He slowly faded into the mist, suffering every single second. |final= {{Collapse|body= |num=Desc |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
Description
{{Underline|ff0000}} The Red Mist is an enigmatic/anomalous level documented in the Backrooms The Red Mist is a combination of different areas and landmarks but it's usually described as a distorted field covered in a thick, crimson mist. The sky has a vivid chartreuse color and the clouds are blood orange with the grass being a dull purple. There is no daytime and nighttime cycle at this level making watches or clocks unable to function. The level is named after its red fog that covers all of the level. The fog also has its own anomalous properties. The mist can cause physical hallucinations of the wanderer's friends, soulmates, family, etc. Most hallucinations in the level involve the wanderer's loved ones being mutilated, shot, stabbed, or any other kind of action that can kill a human being. The mist can also bring back memories and artifacts from the wanderer's childhood and adulthood. Both of these actions will cause the wanderer to have the urge to kill himself/herself to end their suffering. |final= {{Collapse|body= |num=DialougeI |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
Day 4 He woke up... He thought that it would be over He thought he would be in the place he [[The Void|deserves to be]] But no, the level won't let him He walked downstairs, regretting every sin he had done But something was off He remembers this house, and it wasn't the house he first started at It was his Grandmother's house He opened the door to his Grandmother's room The room is empty... |final= {{Collapse|body= |num=DialougeII |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
Day 5 He looked outside... He couldn't see anything except a shadowy blob He squinted... The blob slowly transformed into a blurry tree with two figures being hanged Those two figures were his grandma and his grandfather He wanted to join them He wanted to finally rest from his troubles He headbutts the windows Nothing happened He did it again and again, head pounding harder and harder The window cracked and a shard of glass fell out He grabbed the shard and decided to slit his throat He collapsed with a great thud |final= {{Collapse|body= |num=AreaI |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
Area I (The Field)
{{Underline|ff0000}} The Field is the first and safest section of the level. The area stretches out infinitely and looks similar to [[Level 10]], just with a different hue of colors. All vegetation in this area is extremely poisonous and should not be eaten or consumed, doing so will immediately kill the wanderer. On rare occasions, structures like houses and streets can be found. Most of these building holds small artifacts from the wanderer's past. All the foods and liquids in the houses are expired and share the same effects just like the herbs in the field. Sadly, not much of this area is documented due to its anomalous properties. |final= {{Collapse|body= |num=DialougeIII |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
Day 6 He was still here Still here on this damned level This time he was in a skyscraper Just like that house, he remembers this place It was his [[The Frontrooms|hometown]], New York City "Why I am still here" he screamed "WHY!" he yelled louder Before he can jump off the building, a soldier cries "FIRE" Specks of gold surrounded him below He heard screams of men who were injured badly, screams of men who did good enough to lie in the clouds where they forever rest But why not him He did everything he could to be perfect, he doesn't deserve this The soldiers climbed up the tower he was standing at All carrying sniper rifles willing that they will win the war and reunite with their families Two of the soldiers were his Uncle and Dad They positioned themselves on the rooftop until a loud ''Whiz'' broke all the screams in the fight Before he can even look up at the sky on what's going on, a loud ''BOOM'' rang Everyone was looking at the giant mass of light that arose from the horizon The light transformed into a cloud shaped like a mushroom, he could already tell what was going on He blacked out before the shockwave of the bomb destroyed everything He thought to himself "Is this what's happening to The [[Frontrooms]] while he's gone" All of his hope of exiting this hell is gone... There is nothing to do but to rest in the [[The Void|void]] |final= {{Collapse|body= |num=AreaII |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
Area II (The Memories)
{{Underline|ff0000}} Area II, the memories is the second area of the level. The layout is unique to every wanderer as it's based on the wanderer's memories similar to [[Level 18]]. This is the only area where the mist takes full effect on the wanderer. Instead of creating hallucinations, the mist will completely alter the wanderer's memories about their friends, family, and loved ones to the point where the wanderer believes everything is true. |final= {{Collapse|body= |num=DialougeIV |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
Day ??? He looked around once more He was in a pitch-black void with nowhere to go Until a door opened wide... He walked through the doorway, expecting another reminder of what was going on in [[The Frontrooms]] But he was wrong There was nothing but an infinite field of grass, just like when he first arrived here But this time there was nothing around him The grass was moving lifelessly and the clouds were swirling rapidly in the disappointed sky He got on his knees and cried This was his final resting place |final= {{Collapse|body= |num=EndlougeI |initial=
[[File:The void.png|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
[[File:MIST.jpg|link=|class=backgroundplate]]
There is no entrance... There is no exit... There is no entity... There are no groups either... Just him and the level... Unless he is the entity... He is the one to be exterminated... But he can't, the level won't let him... He's not human either... Then what is he? He found a mirror on the floor, a dirty mirror He stared at it, in shock at what he found It didn't reflect him, instead it pictured a graveyard in [[The Frontrooms]] He glanced at the closest tombstone lying on the field "R.I.P Luke Simons, 1978-1994" |final= |body= }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} }} [[Category:Mental Hazards]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[Category:Format Screws]] [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Anomalous Levels]] [[Category:Content Warning]]
